channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Marquise Williams Will Skip UNC Games Amid Controversy Surrounding Bill Belichick
item.description: Marquise Williams, a former All-ACC quarterback at the University of North Carolina, has announced that he will not attend any Tar Heels football games during the 2026 season. The decision comes as UN...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary John Healey announces consideration of military options to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict
item.description: Defence Secretary John Healey has revealed that the United Kingdom is actively considering ways to assist in defending commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global ...

item.title: Master Wong Arrested Following Robbery Allegations in Kiamaiko
item.description: Online personality Master Wong has been arrested after allegations of robbery surfaced, brought forward by musician Toxic Lyrikali and his associate Mauru Gwash. According to reports, a confrontation ...

item.title: Five Men Face Court Again in Senzo Meyiwa Murder Trial
item.description: The trial of the five men accused of murdering South African soccer star Senzo Meyiwa continues at the Pretoria High Court. Meyiwa, who was the captain of Bafana Bafana, was tragically killed more tha...

item.title: Barrister Adeleke Adeyemo marks 80th birthday by donating church to Iragbiji community
item.description: For Iragbiji, Osun State, na serious celebration happen as eminent lawyer Barrister Adeleke Adeyemo mark him 80th birthday. Him family, friends, colleagues, and community people gather gidigba for him...

item.title: Supreme Court allows passive euthanasia in permanent vegetative state cases
item.description: In a landmark decision, the Supreme Court of India has for the first time permitted passive euthanasia for a patient in a permanent vegetative state, following the case of Harish Rana, who had been co...

item.title: Ontario court dismisses lawsuit targeting The Globe over investigative reporting on alleged foreign interference
item.description: An Ontario Superior Court judge has dismissed a $10-million lawsuit filed by former Ontario Liberal cabinet minister Michael Chan against The Globe and Mail. Chan claimed punitive and personal damages...

item.title: First Nations leaders reaffirm Treaty 6 and Canada's unity at Buckingham Palace
item.description: On March 12, 2026, a delegation of Treaty 6 First Nations chiefs from Alberta and Saskatchewan met King Charles at Buckingham Palace. The meeting coincided with the upcoming 150th anniversary of the T...

item.title: Delhi Introduces GPS-Equipped Tankers to Monitor Water Distribution
item.description: The Delhi government has introduced a new system of GPS-tracked water tankers to improve transparency and efficiency in the supply of water across the city. This initiative aims to tackle the long-sta...

item.title: Independent Commission on Infrastructure submits flood control project documents to Ombudsman
item.description: The Independent Commission on Infrastructure (ICI) recently delivered large volumes of documents regarding its investigation on alleged irregularities in flood control projects to the Office of the Om...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Delays Shipping of Balikbayan Boxes from Dubai
item.description: Overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) sending balikbayan boxes from Dubai are facing delays due to the ongoing Middle East conflict, which has led to the closure of the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Accordin...

item.title: MPs warn over uncertainty in BBC World Service funding and governance
item.description: A House of Commons committee has expressed deep concern over uncertainty surrounding the future funding of the BBC World Service. The government currently provides around 30% of the World Service’s bu...

item.title: How Jenipher Lema is Transforming Tanzania’s Ports through Technology
item.description: Jenipher Lema, IT Application Support Engineer at Tanzania East Africa Gateway Terminal Limited (TEAGTL), is quietly changing how Tanzania’s ports operate using digital technology. She started her fas...

item.title: Rahul questions Modi government’s handling of Iran-US conflict and Russian oil purchase
item.description: In New Delhi, political tensions are rising as India navigates its energy needs amid the escalating conflict in West Asia. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s appa...

item.title: Practical ways to make everyday life more meaningful
item.description: Experts Bill Burnett and Dave Evans, co-founders of the Life Design Lab at Stanford University, suggest that finding meaning in life does not require grand philosophical quests. With traditional sourc...

item.title: Assessing the Consequences of the US-Iran Conflict and Democratic Responsibility
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its 13th day, the human and economic toll is mounting. Nearly 2,000 people have died, including 175 Iranian schoolchildren and seven US service members, with hundreds more in...

item.title: Structural Changes in Blood Proteins Could Enable Early Detection of Alzheimer’s Disease
item.description: Researchers at Scripps Research Institute have developed a novel blood test that may detect Alzheimer’s disease earlier than current methods by analyzing subtle structural changes in proteins rather t...

item.title: René Redzepi resigns from Noma following allegations of physical and psychological abuse by former employees
item.description: René Redzepi, the renowned Danish chef and co-founder of the acclaimed Copenhagen restaurant Noma, has resigned from his leadership role at the establishment. This decision comes in the wake of seriou...

item.title: Rep Anamero Sunday Dekeri Appoints Veteran Broadcaster Benjamin Atu as Media Aide
item.description: The member representing Etsako Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, Honourable Sunday Anamero Dekeri, don appoint veteran broadcaster Benjamin Atu as him media aide. This appointment,...

item.title: Electric Stove Sales Surge Across India As LPG Shortage Deepens
item.description: In many parts of India, households are struggling with an acute shortage of LPG cylinders, which has led to an unprecedented rise in the demand for electric stoves and induction cooktops. With long de...
